methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
NuScale Power operates in the nuclear energy sector, a space encompassing 11 identified peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised over $600M to date and operates at the public stage.

A peer-median valuation approach was not applicable in this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 same-sector comparables, but filtering for same-stage companies, adjacent-stage companies, or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than the three datapoints necessary for a defensible median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material methodological error given the capital intensity and regulatory complexity specific to nuclear energy. No triangulated valuation range can therefore be supported by the available data.

This analysis is constrained by the narrowness of comparable companies in early-stage and development-phase nuclear technology. A credible valuation would require either expansion to adjacent sectors (energy infrastructure, advanced manufacturing) with documented risk adjustments, or reliance on precedent transaction data and discounted cash flow analysis anchored to NuScale's specific project pipeline and regulatory milestones. The 8 private-market peers and 3 public benchmarks remain available for qualitative reference but do not provide sufficient homogeneity for quantitative benchmarking.
